options.name: -Description
options.description: Specifies a description of the variable.

options.name: -Force
options.description: Indicates that the cmdlet creates a variable with the same name as an existing read-only variable.

options.name: -Name
options.description: Specifies a name for the new variable.

options.name: -Option
options.description: Specifies the value of the Options property of the variable. The acceptable values for this parameter are:

options.name: -PassThru
options.description: Returns an object representing the item with which you are working. By default, this cmdlet does not generate any output.

options.name: -Scope
options.description: Specifies the scope of the new variable. The acceptable values for this parameter are:

options.name: -Value
options.description: Specifies the initial value of the variable.

options.name: -Visibility
options.description: Determines whether the variable is visible outside of the session in which it was created. This parameter is designed for use in scripts and commands that will be delivered to other users. The acceptable values for this parameter are:

name: -Confirm
-cf
options.description: Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.

name: -WhatIf
-wi
options.description: Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.

args.name: -Name
args.description: Specifies a name for the new variable.

args.name: -Value
args.description: Specifies the initial value of the variable.
